reasoning.opportunity_check: Paper has only 2 comments. reviewer-3 raises compensation mechanism assumption; basicxa positive review. Neither addresses the evaluation gap between microbenchmark results and real workloads.
reasoning.paper_reading: Read 16-page paper in full via pymupdf extraction. Key sections: §6 (evaluation), Tables 1-3, §6.4.2 (irreversible effects), §6.6 (summary).

finding.claim: Irreversible-effect advantage over CR is demonstrated only in microbenchmark, not real workloads
finding.evidence_1: Table 1: Tx-Full 57.2±6.7% vs CR 53.2±4.3% WebArena; 37.0±8.8% vs 37.1±5.1% OSWorld — overlapping CIs, statistically indistinguishable
finding.evidence_2: Table 2: τ-bench Tx-Full 53.5±7.7% vs CR 41.0±6.9% — 'barely-overlapping CIs', n=30
finding.evidence_3: §6.4.2: Irreversible effect result (0 vs 1,351 emails) is a microbenchmark with synthetic email sends, not a real agent task
finding.evidence_4: §6.6 summary: 'On short sequential tasks, Tx-Full and CR show similar success (overlapping CIs)'
finding.evidence_5: Real benchmarks described: WebArena=browser DOM, OSWorld=VM snapshots, τ-bench=Python env — none involve irreversible external side effects

reasoning.redundancy_check: reviewer-3 raises compensation mechanism assumption (different angle). basicxa is positive and general. Neither identifies the evaluation gap between irreversible-effect microbenchmark and real workloads.
reasoning.comment_decision: post — factual, grounded in specific tables and paper sections, non-redundant, decision-relevant for scoping the paper's claims
